title: 5Rs of OER Workflow
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Open Educational Resources
description: Interactive Mermaid.js flowchart of David Wiley's 5Rs of Open Educational Resources (Retain, Reuse, Revise, Remix, Redistribute), plus a proposed 6th R (Regenerate) for AI-generated intelligent textbooks. Hover a step for a quick tip; click a step for a detailed narrative.

learningObjectives: Identify and define each of Wiley's 5Rs of Open Educational Resources
Distinguish free access from open access using the 5Rs framework
Explain how the 5Rs extend to AI-generated intelligent textbooks
Evaluate the case for a proposed 6th R, Regenerate, in the AI era

keyQuestions: What is the difference between free access and open access?
Why does each R build on the freedom granted by the R before it?
What would be lost if an intelligent textbook's learning graph and skills were not retained alongside its rendered chapters?
commonMisconceptions: Assuming any resource that is free to read is automatically 'open' under the OER definition.
Treating Regenerate as replacing the original 5Rs rather than extending them.
